channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Iran's currency falls to record low against the dollar as tensions run high
item.description: Iran’s rial currency traded at a record low against the U.S. dollar as the country returned to work after a long holiday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korea presidential ouster part of Chinese strategy to 'expand its regional influence,' expert says
item.description: One analyst, as well as supporters of South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol, says China backed Yoon's ouster in South Korea to weaken U.S. ties and expand its influence by propping up Beijing-friendly political allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cambodia's leader presides at ceremony for upgrade of naval base with help from China
item.description: SIHANOUKVILLE, Cambodia (AP) — Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet on Saturday presided over the opening of an expansion of his country’s main naval base, which analysts and the U.S. government suspect will be used as a strategic outpost by China.
item.dc:creator: SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Death toll in Russian missile strike in central Ukraine reaches 18
item.description: The death toll from a Russian missile strike in the central Ukrainian city of Kryvyi Rih has risen to 18, including nine children, regional governor Serhii Lysak said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Argentina's Milei doubles down on Trump bromance as the world reels from trade shock
item.description: While the world’s leaders were wringing their hands over President Donald Trump’s announcement of sweeping tariffs on U.S. imports, Argentina’s right-wing president was ebullient, feted at Trump’s Mar-a-Lago club.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Debre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Yoon Suk Yeol removed as South Korea's president over short-lived martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s Constitutional Court unanimously removed Yoon Suk Yeol from office Friday, ending his tumultuous presidency and setting up a new election, four months after he threw the nation into turmoil with an ill-fated declaration of martial law.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Singer Rara Sekar draws inspiration from nature, encourages people to return to simple living
item.description: OXFORD, England (AP) — Rara Sekar closed her eyes in meditation after performing a song that speaks of rays of light that cut through the fog as one political prisoner faced death more than six decades ago.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA and VICKRAM SOMBU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: England’s Wrestling Church seeks converts with baptisms and body slams
item.description: SHIPLEY, England (AP) — Sitting around a wrestling ring, churchgoers roared as local hero Billy O’Keeffe body-slammed a fighter named Disciple. Beneath stained-glass windows, they whooped and cheered as burly, tattooed wresters tumbled into the aisle during a six-man tag-team battle.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: As stock market continues plummeting over tariffs, Trump spends the day at his golf course
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Two days after sending the economy reeling by announcing widespread tariffs, President Donald Trump insisted his trade policies will never change as he remained ensconced in a bubble of wealth and power in Florida.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, CHRIS MEGERIAN and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Costa Rica looks to El Salvador's gang crackdown for path to stopping violence
item.description: Costa Rica’s security minister has begun a visit to El Salvador to study the measures it has taken to reduce violence caused by its powerful street gangs.
item.dc:creator: Marcos Alemán, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Officials say a Russian strike in central Ukraine kills 14 people, including six children
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian ballistic missile strike Friday on a central Ukrainian city killed at least 14 people, including six children, Ukrainian officials said, as U.S. and European leaders pressed Russia to accept a ceasefire in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Luxury cruise ship caught in 40-foot waves while traveling through Drake Passage
item.description: Passengers on Quark Expeditions' Ocean Explorer ship got rocked with waves up to 40 feet on a recent cruise, rocking those onboard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's tariffs hit uninhabited islands filled with penguins
item.description: The implementation of tariffs on the islands did not go unnoticed by Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Newsom to world: California open for business
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) directed his state to pursue “strategic partnerships” with foreign trading partners and is calling for California-made products to be exempted from the retaliatory tariffs targeting the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump says China 'panicked' with tariff response
item.description: President Trump on Friday chastised China after Beijing responded to major U.S. tariffs with equal duties of its own on U.S. goods. "China played it wrong, they panicked - the one thing they cannot afford to do!" Trump posted on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: In warning to Turkey, Israel hits strategic targets in Syria amid rising tensions
item.description: Israel targets Syrian airbases to warn Turkey against interference, as Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan faces protests at home and ramps up efforts to expand influence in Syria and region as a whole.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump calls for Marine Le Pen to be freed
item.description: President Trump on Thursday defended embattled French far-right leader Marine Le Pen, arguing the “witch hunt” against her is yet another instance of critics in Europe using “lawfare” to quash free speech.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Danish leader tells the US 'you cannot annex another country' as she visits Greenland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — Denmark's prime minister has told the U.S. during a visit to Greenland that “you cannot annex another country,” even with the argument that international security is at stake. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strike on a school in Gaza kills at least 27 people, Palestinian health officials say
item.description: DEIR AL BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes killed at least 100 Palestinians across the Gaza Strip on Thursday, including 27 or more sheltering at a school, according to Palestinian medical authorities, in a stepped-up offensive that Israel’s military said is intended to pressure Hamas and e
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What to expect in South Korea now that Yoon has been removed from office
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The South Korean Constitutional Court’s decision to formally unseat President Yoon Suk Yeol is another test for the country’s democracy after the conservative-liberal divide deepened over his imposition of martial law and subsequent impeachment.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Tunisia, snails inch toward replacing red meat as people turn to cheaper protein
item.description: AKOUDA, Tunisia (AP) — In fields outside their hometown in central Tunisia, an increasing number of unemployed young men are seeking a new way to make a living, picking snails off of rocks and leaves and collecting them in large plastic bags to take to the local market to be sold.
item.dc:creator: MEHDI EL AREM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Haiti taking more steps against gangs after thousands of protesters demand security
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — The leader of Haiti’s transitional presidential council tried to allay surging alarm among Haitians over rampant gang violence by acknowledging Thursday that the country has “become hell for everyone” and promising new measures to stop the bloodshed.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 9 in southwestern Syria
item.description: NAWA, Syria (AP) — Israeli strikes in Syria reportedly killed at least nine people in the southwest of the country on Thursday, as Israel accused Turkey of trying to build a “protectorate” in Syria.
item.dc:creator: MALEK KHATTAB and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US is negotiating a minerals deal with conflict-hit Congo, a Trump official says
item.description: KINSHASA, Congo (AP) — A Trump administration official said Thursday the United States is in talks with conflict-plagued Congo on developing its mineral resources under a deal the Congolese president has said could help make his country safer. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEAN-YVES KAMALE and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Myanmar earthquake death toll rises to 3,145 as more bodies found
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from the earthquake that hit Myanmar nearly a week ago rose Thursday to 3,145 as search and rescue teams found more bodies, the military-led government said, and humanitarian aid groups scrambled to provide survivors medical care and shelter.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump urged to review UN immunity, lax visa rules amid national security concerns
item.description: As the U.S. weighs U.N. funding cuts, experts urge the Trump administration to review the world body's visa process and staff immunity over continued security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada announces 25 percent tariff on non-USMCA compliant US auto imports
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney announced Thursday his country will impose 25 percent tariffs on U.S. auto imports that do not comply with the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) on free trade.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Rubio tries to reassure wary allies of US commitment to NATO as Trump sends mixed signals
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio and the Trump administration’s new envoy to NATO are seeking to reassure wary members of the U.S. commitment to the alliance. Rubio on Thursday decried “hysteria and hyperbole” in the media about U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Slovaks rally to protest against legislation that critics say is harmful to NGOs
item.description: BRATISLAVA, Slovakia (AP) — Thousands marched through Slovakia's capital on Thursday before a planned rally in front of parliament to condemn draft legislation that critics consider detrimental for many nongovernmental organizations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most Americans oppose deporting migrants to El Salvador without due process: Survey
item.description: A majority of Americans oppose deporting migrants who have not been convicted to El Salvador without due process, according to a new survey, as the Trump administration defends its deportation efforts amid broad scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump levies tariffs on uninhabited islands
item.description: President Trump, as part of his expansive package of new tariffs, levied taxes on a number of uninhabited or sparsely populated islands that have little-to-no exports. The announcement of new tariffs on Wednesday will hit nearly all foreign countries with a baseline tax at 10 percent.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Ontario premier ‘cautiously optimistic’ amid Trump tariffs
item.description: “Make no mistake about it, Canadians love Americans. They love the U.S., and I do, too,” Doug Ford said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariffs likely to spur recession: Moody's economist
item.description: Mark Zandi said the situation may not be as grave if countries do not respond “in kind” to the president’s tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: “Tragically, three soldiers were found deceased in Lithuania yesterday, and it pains me to confirm that today the fourth soldier was also found deceased,” White House said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu accuses Israeli police of trying to 'topple' his government
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is outraged over the ongoing Qatar probe and says it is a political investigation that is meant to prevent the firing of Shin Bet chief Ronen Bar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistan plans to expel 3 million Afghans from the country this year
item.description: Pakistan plans to expel 3 million Afghans from the country this year, as a deadline for them to voluntarily leave the capital and surrounding areas expired on Monday.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN and RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: MANDALAY, Myanmar (AP) — The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar's second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and le
item.dc:creator: THEIN ZAW, DAVID RISING and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, WGN 9
